chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D-1207 of 2026 (Baseer Ahmed v. Province of Sindh through A.G Sindh and others) __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Saleem Jessar Mr. Justice Nisar Ahmed Bhanbhro Fresh case 1. For orders on Misc. No.4609/2026 (Urgent Application) 2. For orders on Misc. No.4610/2026 (Exemption Application) 3. For orders on Misc. No.4611/2026 (Stay Application) 4. For hearing of main case 25.02.2026 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, Advocate for the petitioner ---------------------- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 3&4. Through this petition the petitioner has claimed following relief:- 1. Grant protective / transitory bail to the Petitioner in respect of any other FIR(s), if already registered or which may be registered, known or unknown, arising out of or relating to the same disputed cheques / cheque transaction, strictly for a limited period, to protect the Petitioner from arrest and to enable him to avail lawful remedies before the competent forum. 2. Direct that during the currency of such protective/transitory bail, the Petitioner shall not be arrested or harassed in any criminal proceedings arising out of the same disputed cheques, subject to his cooperation with investigation and appearance before the competent court. 3. Direct the Respondent police officials not to arrest the Petitioner in any criminal matter arising out of the same cheque transaction, at least till the decision of the pending civil suit. 4. Direct the Respondent police authorities that if any FIR(s) exist or are lodged relating to the said transaction, the same be produced before this Honourable Court. 5.

Direct the Respondent police officials not to arrest the Petitioner in any criminal matter arising out of the same cheque transaction, at least till the decision of the pending civil suit. 4. Direct the Respondent police authorities that if any FIR(s) exist or are lodged relating to the said transaction, the same be produced before this Honourable Court. 5. Direct Respondent Banks Nos. 10 to 12 not to dishonour the disputed cheques or issue dishonour memos till final decision of the pending civil suit.” It is the case of the petitioner that he has issued post-dated cheques and apprehends that on presentation same may be Const.Petition No.D-1207/2026 -2- dishonored and criminal case(s) will be registered against him under Section 489-F PPC. It transpires from the record that the petitioner has filed the suit seeking similar relief through Civil Suit No.95 of 2026 before the Court of Senior Civil Judge, Malir, Karachi. When confronted as to maintainability of this petition, counsel for the petitioner failed to justify in filing of multiple litigations on the same subject matter before different forums. It is settled proposition of law that a person has to choose a particular forum to address his grievance. Since the petitioner has already filed civil suit therefore was precluded from filing the parallel litigation. Even otherwise, the relief claimed through the prayer made in the instant petition, cannot be granted to the petitioner on the ground of apprehension. In the wake of foregoing discussion, this petition is dismissed in limine alongwith pending application. JUDGE HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE B-K Soomro
